title: Epitaph
content:
Heap not on this mound
                 Roses that she loved so well;
             Why bewilder her with roses,
                 That she cannot see or smell?
             She is happy where she lies
                 With the dust upon her eyes.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edna St. Vincent Millay, titled "Epitaph", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
